title: The Oration on Holy Baptism.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Preached at Constantinople Jan.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: 6, 381, being the day following the delivery of that on the Holy Lights.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yesterday we kept high Festival on the illustrious Day of the Holy Lights; for it was fitting that rejoicings should be kept for our Salvation, and that far more than for weddings and birthdays, and namedays, and house-warmings, and registrations of children, and anniversaries, and all the other festivities that men observe for their earthly friends.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And now to-day let us discourse briefly concerning Baptism, and the benefits which accrue to us therefrom, even though our discourse yesterday spoke of it cursorily; partly because the time pressed us hard, and partly because the sermon had to avoid tediousness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For too great length in a sermon is as much an enemy to people’s ears, as too much food is to their bodies.…It will be worth your while to apply your minds to what we say, and to receive our discourse on so important a subject not perfunctorily, but with ready mind, since to know the power of this Sacrament is itself Enlightenment.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Word recognizes three Births for us; namely, the natural birth, that of Baptism, and that of the Resurrection.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Of these the first is by night, and is servile, and involves passion; but the second is by day, and is destructive of passion, cutting off all the veil that is derived from birth, and leading on to the higher life; and the third is more terrible and shorter, bringing together in a moment all mankind, to stand before its Creator, and to give an account of its service and conversation here; whether it has followed the flesh, or whether it has mounted up with the spirit, and worshipped the grace of its new creation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: My Lord Jesus Christ has showed that He honoured all these births in His own Person; the first, by that first and quickening Inbreathing; the second by His Incarnation and the Baptism wherewith He Himself was baptized; and the third by the Resurrection of which He was the Firstfruits; condescending, as He became the Firstborn among many brethren, so also to become the Firstborn from the dead.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Concerning two of these births, the first and the last, we have not to speak on the present occasion.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us discourse upon the second, which is now necessary for us, and which gives its name to the Feast of the Lights.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Illumination is the splendour of souls, the conversion of the life, the question put to the Godward conscience.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is the aid to our weakness, the renunciation of the flesh, the following of the Spirit, the fellowship of the Word, the improvement of the creature, the overwhelming of sin, the participation of light, the dissolution of darkness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is the carriage to God, the dying with Christ, the perfecting of the mind, the bulwark of Faith, the key of the Kingdom of heaven, the change of life, the removal of slavery, the loosing of chains, the remodelling of the whole man.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why should I go into further detail?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Illumination is the greatest and most magnificent of the Gifts of God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For just as we speak of the Holy of Holies, and the Song of Songs, as more comprehensive and more excellent than others, so is this called Illumination, as being more holy than any other illumination which we possess.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And as Christ the Giver of it is called by many various names, so too is this Gift, whether it is from the exceeding gladness of its nature (as those who are very fond of a thing take pleasure in using its name), or that the great variety of its benefits has reacted for us upon its names.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We call it, the Gift, the Grace, Baptism, Unction, Illumination, the Clothing of Immortality, the Laver of Regeneration, the Seal, and everything that is honourable.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We call it the Gift, because it is given to us in return for nothing on our part; Grace, because it is conferred even on debtors; Baptism, because sin is buried with it in the water; Unction, as Priestly and Royal, for such were they who were anointed; Illumination, because of its splendour; Clothing, because it hides our shame; the Laver, because it washes us; the Seal because it preserves us, and is moreover the indication of Dominion.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In it the heavens rejoice; it is glorified by Angels, because of its kindred splendour.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is the image of the heavenly bliss.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We long indeed to sing out its praises, but we cannot worthily do so.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: God is Light: the highest, the unapproachable, the ineffable, That can neither be conceived in the mind nor uttered with the lips, That giveth life to every reasoning creature.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He is in the world of thought, what the sun is in the world of sense; presenting Himself to our minds in proportion as we are cleansed; and loved in proportion as He is presented to our mind; and again, conceived in proportion as we love Him; Himself contemplating and comprehending Himself, and pouring Himself out upon what is external to Him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: That Light, I mean, which is contemplated in the Father and the Son and the Holy Ghost, Whose riches is Their unity of nature, and the one outleaping of Their brightness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: A second Light is the Angel, a kind of outflow or communication of that first Light, drawing its illumination from its inclination and obedience thereto; and I know not whether its illumination is distributed according to the order of its state, or whether its order is due to the respective measures of its illumination.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: A third Light is man; a light which is visible to external objects.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For they call man light because of the faculty of speech in us.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And the name is applied again to those of us who are more like God, and who approach God more nearly than others.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I also acknowledge another Light, by which the primeval darkness was driven away or pierced.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It was the first of all the visible creation to be called into existence; and it irradiates the whole universe, the circling orbit of the stars, and all the heavenly beacon fires.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Light was also the firstborn commandment given to the firstborn man (for the commandment of the Law is a lamp and a light; and again, Because Thy judgments are a light upon the earth); although the envious darkness crept in and wrought wickedness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And a Light typical and proportionate to those who were its subjects was the written law, adumbrating the truth and the sacrament of the great Light, for Moses’ face was made glorious by it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And, to mention more Lights—it was Light that appeared out of Fire to Moses, when it burned the bush indeed, but did not consume it, to shew its nature and to declare the power that was in it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And it was Light that was in the pillar of fire that led Israel and tamed the wilderness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It was Light that carried up Elias in the car of fire, and yet did not burn him as it carried him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It was Light that shone round the Shepherds when the Eternal Light was mingled with the temporal.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It was Light that was the beauty of the Star that went before to Bethlehem to guide the Wise Men’s way, and to be the escort of the Light That is above us, when He came amongst us.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Light was That Godhead Which was shewn upon the Mount to the disciples—and a little too strong for their eyes.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Light was That Vision which blazed out upon Paul, and by wounding his eyes healed the darkness of his soul.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Light is also the brilliancy of heaven to those who have been purified here, when the righteous shall shine forth as the Sun, and God shall stand in the midst of them, gods and kings, deciding and distinguishing the ranks of the Blessedness of heaven.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Light beside these in a special sense is the illumination of Baptism of which we are now speaking; for it contains a great and marvellous sacrament of our salvation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For since to be utterly sinless belongs to God, and to the first and uncompounded nature (for simplicity is peaceful, and not subject to dissension), and I venture to say also that it belongs to the Angelic nature too; or at least, I would affirm that nature to be very nearly sinless, because of its nearness to God; but to sin is human and belongs to the Compound on earth (for composition is the beginning of separation); therefore the master did not think it right to leave His creature unaided, or to neglect its danger of separation from Himself; but on the contrary, just as He gave existence to that which did not exist, so He gave new creation to that which did exist, a diviner creation and a loftier than the first, which is to those who are beginning life a Seal, and to those who are more mature in age both a gift and a restoration of the image which had fallen through sin, that we may not, by becoming worse through despair, and ever being borne downward to that which is more evil, fall altogether from good and from virtue, through despondency; and having fallen into a depth of evil (as it is said) despise Him; but that like those who in the course of a long journey make a brief rest from labour at an inn, we should be enabled to accomplish the rest of the road fresh and full of courage.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such is the grace and power of baptism; not an overwhelming of the world as of old, but a purification of the sins of each individual, and a complete cleansing from all the bruises and stains of sin.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And since we are double-made, I mean of body and soul, and the one part is visible, the other invisible, so the cleansing also is twofold, by water and the spirit; the one received visibly in the body, the other concurring with it invisibly and apart from the body; the one typical, the other real and cleansing the depths.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And this which comes to the aid of our first birth, makes us new instead of old, and like God instead of what we now are; recasting us without fire, and creating us anew without breaking us up.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For, to say it all in one word, the virtue of Baptism is to be understood as a covenant with God for a second life and a purer conversation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And indeed all need to fear this very much, and to watch our own souls, each one of us, with all care, that we do not become liars in respect of this profession.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For if God is called upon as a Mediator to ratify human professions, how great is the danger if we be found transgressors of the covenant which we have made with God Himself; and if we be found guilty before the Truth Himself of that lie, besides our other transgressions…and that when there is no second regeneration, or recreation, or restoration to our former state, even though we seek it with all our might, and with many sighs and tears, by which it is cicatrized over (with great difficulty in my opinion, though we all believe that it may be cicatrized).

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yet if we might wipe away even the scars I should be glad, since I too have need of mercy.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But it is better not to stand in need of a second cleansing, but to stop at the first, which is, I know, common to all, and involves no labour, and is of equal price to slaves, to masters, to poor, to rich, to humble, to exalted, to gentle, to simple, to debtors, to those who are free from debt; like the breathing of the air, and the pouring forth of the light, and the changes of the seasons, and the sight of creation, that great delight which we all share alike, and the equal distribution of the faith.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is a strange thing to substitute for a painless remedy one which is more painful; to cast away the grace of mercy, and owe a debt of punishment; and to measure our amendment against sin.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For how many tears must we contribute before they can equal the fount of baptism; and who will be surety for us that death shall wait for our cure, and that the judgment seat shall not summon us while still debtors, and needing the fire of the other world?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You perhaps, as a good and pitiful husbandman, will entreat the Master still to spare the figtree, and not yet to cut it down, though accused of unfruitfulness; but to allow you to put dung about it in the shape of tears, sighs, invocations, sleepings on the ground, vigils, mortifications of soul and body, and correction by confession and a life of humiliation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But it is uncertain if the Master will spare it, inasmuch as it cumbers the ground of another asking for mercy, and becoming deteriorated by the longsuffering shewn to this one.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us then be buried with Christ by Baptism, that we may also rise with Him; let us descend with Him, that we may also be exalted with Him; let us ascend with Him, that we may also be glorified together.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If after baptism the persecutor and tempter of the light assail you (for he assailed even the Word my God through the veil, the hidden Light through that which was manifested), you have the means to conquer him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Fear not the conflict; defend yourself with the Water; defend yourself with the Spirit, by Which all the fiery darts of the wicked shall be quenched.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is Spirit, but That Spirit which rent the Mountains.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is Water, but that which quenches fire.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If he assail you by your want (as he dared to assail Christ), and asks that stones should be made bread, do not be ignorant of his devices.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Teach him what he has not learnt.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Defend yourself with the Word of life, Who is the Bread sent down from heaven, and giving life to the world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If he plot against you with vain glory (as he did against Christ when he led Him up to the pinnacle of the temple and said to Him, Cast Thyself down as a proof of Thy Godhead), be not overborne by elation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you be taken by this he will not stop here.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For he is insatiable, he grasps at every thing.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He fawns upon you with fair pretences, but he ends in evil; this is the manner of his fighting.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yes, and the robber is skilled in Scripture.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: On the one side was that It is written about the Bread, and on the other that it Is written about the Angels.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is written, quoth he, He shall give His Angels charge concerning thee, and they shall bear thee in their hands.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: O vile sophist! how was it that thou didst suppress the words that follow, for I know it well, even if thou passest it by in silence?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will make thee to go upon the asp and basilisk, and I will tread upon serpents and scorpions, being fenced by the Trinity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If he wrestle against thee to a fall through avarice, shewing thee all the Kingdoms at one instant and in the twinkling of an eye, as belonging to himself, and demand thy worship, despise him as a beggar.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Say to him relying on the Seal, “I am myself the Image of God; I have not yet been cast down from the heavenly Glory, as thou wast through thy pride; I have put on Christ; I have been transformed into Christ by Baptism; worship thou me.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Well do I know that he will depart, defeated and put to shame by this; as he did from Christ the first Light, so he will from those who are illumined by Christ.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such blessings does the laver bestow on those who apprehend it; such is the rich feast which it provides for those who hunger aright.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us then be baptized that we may win the victory; let us partake of the cleansing waters, more purifying than hyssop, purer than the legal blood, more sacred than the ashes of the heifer sprinkling the unclean, and providing a temporary cleansing of the body, but not a complete taking away of sin; for if once purged, why should they need further purification?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us be baptized today, that we suffer not violence to-morrow; and let us not put off the blessing as if it were an injury, nor wait till we get more wicked that more may be forgiven us; and let us not become sellers and traffickers of Christ, lest we become more heavily burdened than we are able to bear, that we be not sunk with all hands and make shipwreck of the Gift, and lose all because we expected too much.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: While thou art still master of thy thoughts run to the Gift.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: While thou art not yet sick in body or in mind, nor seemest so to those who are with thee (though thou art really of sound mind); while thy good is not yet in the power of others, but thou thyself art still master of it; while thy tongue is not stammering or parched, or (to say no more) deprived of the power of pronouncing the sacramental words; while thou canst still be made one of the faithful, not conjecturally but confessedly; and canst still receive not pity but congratulation; while the Gift is still clear to thee, and there is no doubt about it; while the grace can reach the depth of thy soul, and it is not merely thy body that is washed for burial; and before tears surround thee announcing thy decease—and even these restrained perhaps for thy sake—and thy wife and children would delay thy departure, and are listening for thy dying words; before the physician is powerless to help thee, and is giving thee but hours to live—hours which are not his to give—and is balancing thy salvation with the nod of his head, and discoursing learnedly on thy disease after thou art dead, or making his charges heavier by withdrawals, or hinting at despair; before there is a struggle between the man who would baptize thee and the man who seeks thy money, the one striving that thou mayest receive thy Viaticum, the other that he may be inscribed in thy Will as heir—and there is no time for both.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why wait for a fever to bring you this blessing, and refuse it from God?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why will you have it through lapse of time, and not through reason?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why will you owe it to a plotting friend, and not to a saving desire?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why will you receive it of force and not of free will; of necessity rather than of liberty?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why must you hear of your death from another, rather than think of it as even now present?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why do you seek for drugs which will do no good, or the sweat of the crisis, when the sweat of death is perhaps upon you?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Heal yourself before your extremity; have pity upon yourself the only true healer of your disease; apply to yourself the really saving medicine; while you are still sailing with a favouring breeze fear shipwreck, and you will be in less danger of it, if you make use of your terror as a helper.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Give yourself occasion to celebrate the Gift with feasting, not with mourning; let the talent be cultivated, not buried in the ground; let some time intervene between the grace and death, that not only may the account of sins be wiped out, but something better may be written in its place; that you may have not only the Gift, but also the Reward; that you may not only escape the fire, but may also inherit the glory, which is bestowed by cultivation of the Gift.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For to men of little soul it is a great thing to escape torment; but men of great soul aim also at attaining reward.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I know of three classes among the saved; the slaves, the hired servants, the sons.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you are a slave, be afraid of the whip; if you are a hired servant, look only to receive your hire; if you are more than this, a son, revere Him as a Father, and work that which is good, because it is good to obey a Father; and even though no reward should come of it for you, this is itself a reward, that you please your Father.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us then take care not to despise these things.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How absurd it would be to grasp at money and throw away health; and to be lavish of the cleansing of the body, but economical over the cleansing of the soul; and to seek for freedom from earthly slavery, but not to care about heavenly freedom; and to make every effort to be splendidly housed and dressed, but to have never a thought how you yourself may become really very precious; and to be zealous to do good to others, without any desire to do good to yourself.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if good could be bought, you would spare no money; but if mercy is freely at your feet, you despise it for its cheapness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Every time is suitable for your ablution, since any time may be your death.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: With Paul I shout to you with that loud voice, “Behold now is the accepted time; behold Now is the day of salvation;” and that Now does not point to any one time, but is every present moment.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And again “Awake, thou that sleepest, and Christ shall give thee light,” dispelling the darkness of sin.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For as Isaiah says, In the night hope is evil, and it is more profitable to be received in the morning.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Sow in good season, and gather together, and open thy barns when it is the time to do so; and plant in season, and let the clusters be cut when they are ripe, and launch boldly in spring, and draw thy ship on shore again at the beginning of winter, when the sea begins to rage.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And let there be to thee also a time for war and a time for peace; a time to marry, and a time to abstain from marrying; a time for friendship, and a time for discord, if this be needed; and in short a time for everything, if you will follow Solomon’s advice.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And it is best to do so, for the advice is profitable.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But the work of your salvation is one upon which you should be engaged at all times; and let every time be to you the definite one for Baptism.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you are always passing over to-day and waiting for to-morrow, by your little procrastinations you will be cheated without knowing it by the Evil One, as his manner is.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Give to me, he says, the present, and to God the future; to me your youth, and to God old age; to me your pleasures, and to Him your uselessness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How great is the danger that surrounds you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How many the unexpected mischances.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: War has expended you; or an earthquake overwhelmed you; or the sea swallowed you up; or a wild beast carried you off; or a sickness killed you; or a crumb going the wrong way (a most insignificant thing, but what is easier than for a man to die, though you are so proud of the divine image); or a too freely indulged drinking bout; or a wind knocked you down; or a horse ran away with you; or a drug maliciously scheming against you, or perhaps found to be deleterious when meant to be wholesome; or an inhuman judge; or an inexorable executioner; or any of the things which make the change swiftest and beyond the power of human aid.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if you would fortify yourself beforehand with the Seal, and secure yourself for the future with the best and strongest of all aids, being signed both in body and in soul with the unction, as Israel was of old with that blood and unction of the firstborn at night that guarded him, what then can happen to you, and what has been wrought out for you?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Listen to the Proverbs.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: “If thou sittest, he says, thou shalt be without fear; and if thou sleepest, thy sleep shall be sweet.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And listen to David giving thee the good news, “Thou shalt not be afraid for the terror by night, for mischance or noonday demon.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This, even while you live, will greatly contribute to your sense of safety (for a sheep that is sealed is not easily snared, but that which is unmarked is an easy prey to thieves), and at your death a fortunate shroud, more precious than gold, more magnificent than a sepulchre, more reverent than fruitless libations, more seasonable than ripe firstfruits, which the dead bestow on the dead, making a law out of custom.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Nay, if all things forsake thee, or be taken violently away from thee; money, possessions, thrones, distinctions, and everything that belongs to this early turmoil, yet you will be able to lay down your life in safety, having suffered no loss of the helps which God gave you unto salvation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But are you afraid lest you should destroy the Gift, and do you therefore put off your cleansing, because you cannot have it a second time?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Would you not be afraid of danger in time of persecution, and of losing the most precious Thing you have—Christ?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Would you then on this account avoid becoming a Christian?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Perish the thought.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such a fear is not for a sane man; such an argument argues insanity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: O incautious caution, if I may so.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: O trick of the Evil One!

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Truly he is darkness and pretends to be light; and when he can no longer prevail in open war, he lays snares in secret, and gives advice, apparently good, really evil, if by some trick at least he may prevail, and we find no escape from his plotting.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And this is clearly what he is aiming at in this instance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For, being unable to persuade you to despise Baptism, he inflicts loss upon you through a fictitious security; that in consequence of your fear you may suffer unconsciously the very thing you are afraid of; and because you fear to destroy the Gift, you may for this very reason fail of the Gift altogether.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This is his character; and he will never cease his duplicity as long as he sees us pressing onwards towards heaven from which he has fallen.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Wherefore, O man of God, do thou recognize the plots of thine adversary; for the battle is against him that hath, and it is concerned with the most important interests.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Take not thine enemy to be thy counsellor; despise not to be and to be called Faithful.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: As long as you are a Catechumen you are but in the porch of Religion; you must come inside, and cross the court, and observe the Holy Things, and look into the Holy of Holies, and be in company with the Trinity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Great are the interests for which you are fighting, great too the stability which you need.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Protect yourself with the shield of faith.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He fears you, if you fight armed with this weapon, and therefore he would strip you of the Gift, that he may the more easily overcome you unarmed and defenceless.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He assails every age, and every form of life; he must be repelled by all.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Art thou young? stand against thy passions; be numbered with the alliance in the army of God: do valiantly against Goliath.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Take your thousands or your myriads; thus enjoy your manhood; but do not allow your youth to be withered, being killed by the imperfection of your faith.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Are you old and near the predestined necessity?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Aid your few remaining days.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Entrust the purification to your old age.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why do you fear youthful passion in deep old age and at your last breath?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or will you wait to be washed till you are dead, and not so much the object of pity as of dislike?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Are you regretting the dregs of pleasure, being yourself in the dregs of life?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is a shameful thing to be past indeed the flower of your age, but not past your wickedness; but either to be involved in it still, or at least to seem so by delaying your purification.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Have you an infant child?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not let sin get any opportunity, but let him be sanctified from his childhood; from his very tenderest age let him be consecrated by the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Fearest thou the Seal on account of the weakness of nature?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: O what a small-souled mother, and of how little faith!

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why, Anna even before Samuel was born promised him to God, and after his birth consecrated him at once, and brought him up in the priestly habit, not fearing anything in human nature, but trusting in God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You have no need of amulets or incantations, with which the Devil also comes in, stealing worship from God for himself in the minds of vainer men.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Give your child the Trinity, that great and noble Guard.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Are you living in Virginity?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Be sealed by this purification; make this the sharer and companion of your life.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let this direct your life, your words, every member, every movement, every sense.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Honour it, that it may honour you; that it may give to your head a crown of graces, and with a crown of delights may shield you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Art thou bound by wedlock?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Be bound also by the Seal; make it dwell with you as a guardian of your continence, safer than any number of eunuchs or of doorkeepers.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Art thou not yet wedded to flesh?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Fear not this consecration; thou art pure even after marriage.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will take the risk of that.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will join you in wedlock.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will dress the bride.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We do not dishonour marriage because we give a higher honour to virginity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will imitate Christ, the pure Grooms-man and Bridegroom, as He both wrought a miracle at a wedding, and honours wedlock with His Presence.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Only let marriage be pure and unmingled with filthy lusts.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This only I ask; receive safety from the Gift, and give to the Gift the oblation of chastity in its due season, when the fixed time of prayer comes round, and that which is more precious than business.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And do this by common consent and approval.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For we do not command, we exhort; and we would receive something of you for your own profit, and the common security of you both.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And in one word, there is no state of life and no occupation to which Baptism is not profitable.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You who are a free man, be curbed by it; you who are in slavery, be made of equal rank; you who are in grief, receive comfort; let the gladsome be disciplined; the poor receive riches that cannot be taken away; the rich be made capable of being good stewards of their possessions.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not play tricks or lay plots against your own salvation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For even if we can delude others we cannot delude ourselves.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And so to play against oneself is very dangerous and foolish.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But you have to live in the midst of public affairs, and are stained by them; and it would be a terrible thing to waste this mercy.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The answer is simple.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Flee, if you can, even from the forum, along with the good company, making yourself the wings of an eagle, or, to speak more suitably, of a dove…for what have you to do with Cæsar or the things of Cæsar?…until you can rest where there is no sin, and no blackening, and no biting snake in the way to hinder your godly steps.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Snatch your soul away from the world; flee from Sodom; flee from the burning; travel on without turning back, lest you should be fixed as a pillar of salt.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Escape to the Mountain lest you be destroyed with the plain.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if you are already bound and constrained by the chain of necessity, reason thus with yourself; or rather let me reason thus with you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is better both to attain the good and to keep the purification.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if it be impossible to do both it is surely better to be a little stained with your public affairs than to fall altogether short of grace; just as I think it better to undergo a slight punishment from father or master than to be put out of doors; and to be a little beamed upon than to be left in total darkness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And it is the part of wise men to choose, as in good things the greater and more perfect, so in evils the lesser and lighter.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Wherefore do not overmuch dread the purification.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For our success is always judged by comparison with our place in life by our just and merciful Judge; and often one who is in public life and has had small success has had a greater reward than one who in the enjoyment of liberty has not completely succeeded; as I think it more marvellous for a man to advance a little in fetters, than for one to run who is not carrying any weight; or to be only a little spattered in walking through mud, than to be perfectly clean when the road is clean.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To give you a proof of what I have said:—Rahab the harlot was justified by one thing alone, her hospitality, though she receives no praise for the rest of her conduct; and the Publican was exalted by one thing, his humility, though he received no testimony for anything else; so that you may learn not easily to despair concerning yourself.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But some will say, What shall I gain, if, when I am preoccupied by baptism, and have cut off myself by my haste from the pleasures of life, when it was in my power to give the reins to pleasure, and then to obtain grace?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the labourers in the vineyard who had worked the longest time gained nothing thereby, for equal wages were given to the very last.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You have delivered me from some trouble, whoever you are who say this, because you have at last with much difficulty told the secret of your delay; and though I cannot applaud your shiftiness, I do applaud your confession.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But come hither and listen to the interpretation of the parable, that you may not be injured by Scripture for want of information.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: First of all, there is no question here of baptism, but of those who believe at different times and enter the good vineyard of the Church.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For from the day and hour at which each believed, from that day and hour he is required to work.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And then, although they who entered first contributed more to the measure of the labour yet they did not contribute more to the measure of the purpose; nay perhaps even more was due to the last in respect of this, though the statement may seem paradoxical.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the cause of their later entrance was their later call to the work of the vineyard.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In all other respects let us see how different they are.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The first did not believe or enter till they had agreed on their hire; but the others came forward to do the work without an agreement, which is a proof of greater faith.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And the first were found to be of an envious and murmuring nature, but no such charge is brought against the others.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And to the first, that which was given was wages, though they were worthless fellows; to the last it was the free gift.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So that the first were convicted of folly, and with reason deprived of the greater reward.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us see what would have happened to them if they had been late.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why, the equal pay, evidently.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How then can they blame the employer as unjust because of their equality?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For all these things take away the merit of their labour from the first, although they were at work first; and therefore it turns out that the distribution of equal pay was just, if you measure the good will against the labour.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But supposing that the Parable does sketch the power of the font according to your interpretation, what would prevent you, if you entered first, and bore the heat, from avoiding envy of the last, that by this very lovingkindness you might obtain more, and receive the reward, not as of grace but as of debt?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And next, the workmen who receive the wages are those who have entered, not those who have missed, the vineyard; which last is like to be your case.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So that if it were certain that you would obtain the Gift, though you are of such a mind, and maliciously keep back some of the labour, you might be forgiven for taking refuge in such arguments, and desiring to make unlawful gain out of the kindness of the master; though I might assure you that the very fact of being able to labour is a greater reward to any who is not altogether of a huckstering mind.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But since there is a risk of your being altogether shut out of the vineyard through your bargaining, and losing the capital through stopping to pick up little gains, do let yourselves be persuaded by my words to forsake the false interpretations and contradictions, and to come forward without arguing to receive the Gift, lest you should be snatched away before you realize your hopes, and should find out that it was to your own loss that you devised these sophistries.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But then, you say, is not God merciful, and since He knows our thoughts and searches out our desires, will He not take the desire of Baptism instead of Baptism?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You are speaking in riddles, if what you mean is that because of God’s mercy the unenlightened is enlightened in His sight; and he is within the kingdom of heaven who merely desires to attain to it, but refrains from doing that which pertains to the kingdom.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will, however, speak out boldly my opinion on these matters; and I think that all other sensible men will range themselves on my side.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Of those who have received the gift, some were altogether alien from God and from salvation, both addicted to all manner of sin, and desirous to be bad; others were semivicious, and in a kind of mean state between good and bad; others again, while they did that which was evil, yet did not approve their own action, just as men in a fever are not pleased with their own sickness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And others even before they were illuminated were worthy of praise; partly by nature, and partly by the care with which they prepared themselves for Baptism.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: These after their initiation became evidently better, and less liable to fall; in the one case with a view to procuring good, and in the other in order to preserve it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And amongst these, those who gave in to some evil are better than those who were altogether bad; and better still than those who yielded a little, are those who were more zealous, and broke up their fallow ground before Baptism; they have the advantage over the others of having already laboured; for the font does not do away with good deeds as it does with sins.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But better even than these are they who are also cultivating the Gift, and are polishing themselves to the utmost possible beauty.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And so also in those who fail to receive the Gift, some are altogether animal or bestial, according as they are either foolish or wicked; and this, I think, has to be added to their other sins, that they have no reverence at all for this Gift, but look upon it as a mere gift—to be acquiesced in if given them, and if not given them, then to be neglected.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Others know and honour the Gift, but put it off; some through laziness, some through greediness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Others are not in a position to receive it, perhaps on account of infancy, or some perfectly involuntary circumstance through which they are prevented from receiving it, even if they wish.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: As then in the former case we found much difference, so too in this.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: They who altogether despise it are worse than they who neglect it through greed or carelessness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: These are worse than they who have lost the Gift through ignorance or tyranny, for tyranny is nothing but an involuntary error.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And I think that the first will have to suffer punishment, as for all their sins, so for their contempt of baptism; and that the second will also have to suffer, but less, because it was not so much through wickedness as through folly that they wrought their failure; and that the third will be neither glorified nor punished by the righteous Judge, as unsealed and yet not wicked, but persons who have suffered rather than done wrong.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For not every one who is not bad enough to be punished is good enough to be honoured; just as not every one who is not good enough to be honoured is bad enough to be punished.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And I look upon it as well from another point of view.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you judge the murderously disposed man by his will alone, apart from the act of murder, then you may reckon as baptized him who desired baptism apart from the reception of baptism.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if you cannot do the one how can you do the other?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I cannot see it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or, if you like, we will put it thus:—If desire in your opinion has equal power with actual baptism, then judge in the same way in regard to glory, and you may be content with longing for it, as if that were itself glory.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And what harm is done you by your not attaining the actual glory, as long as you have the desire for it?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Therefore since you have heard these words, come forward to it, and be enlightened, and your faces shall not be ashamed through missing the Grace.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Receive then the Enlightenment in due season, that darkness pursue you not, and catch you, and sever you from the Illumining.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The night cometh when no man can work after our departure hence.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The one is the voice of David, the other of the True Light which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And consider how Solomon reproves you who are too idle or lethargic, saying, How long wilt thou sleep, O sluggard, and when wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You rely upon this or that, and “pretend pretences in sins;” am waiting for Epiphany; I prefer Easter; I will wait for Pentecost.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is better to be baptized with Christ, to rise with Christ on the Day of His Resurrection, to honour the Manifestation of the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And what then?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The end will come suddenly in a day for which thou lookest not, and in an hour that thou art not aware of; and then you will have for a companion lack of grace; and you will be famished in the midst of all those riches of goodness, though you ought to reap the opposite fruit from the opposite course, a harvest by diligence, and refreshment from the font, like the thirsty hart that runs in haste to the spring, and quenches the labour of his race by water; and not to be in Ishmael’s case, dried up for want of water, or as the fable has it, punished by thirst in the midst of a spring.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is a sad thing to let the market day go by and then to seek for work.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is a sad thing to let the Manna pass and then to long for food.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is a sad thing to take a counsel too late, and to become sensible of the loss only when it is impossible to repair it; that is, after our departure hence, and the bitter closing of the acts of each man’s life, and the punishment of sinners, and the glory of the purified.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Therefore do not delay in coming to grace, but hasten, lest the robber outstrip you, lest the adulterer pass you by, lest the insatiate be satisfied before you, lest the murderer seize the blessing first, or the publican or the fornicator, or any of these violent ones who take the Kingdom of heaven by force.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it suffers violence willingly, and is tyrannized over through goodness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Take my advice, my friend, and be slow to do evil, but swift to your salvation; for readiness to evil and tardiness to good are equally bad.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you are invited to a revel, be not swift to go; if to apostasy, leap away; if a company of evildoers say to you, “Come with us, share our bloodguiltiness, let us hide in the earth a righteous man unjustly,” do not lend them even your ears.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Thus you will make two very great gains; you will make known to the other his sin, and you will deliver yourself from evil company.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if David the Great say unto you, Come and let us rejoice in the Lord; or another Prophet, Come and let us ascend into the Mountain of the Lord; or our Saviour Himself, Come unto me all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest; or, Arise, let us go hence, shining brightly, glittering above snow, whiter than milk, shining above the sapphire stone; let us not resist or delay.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us be like Peter and John, and let us hasten; as they did to the Sepulchre and the Resurrection, so we to the Font; running together, racing against each other, striving to be first to obtain this Blessing.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And say not, “Go away, and come again, and tomorrow I will be baptized,” when you may have the blessing today.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: “I will have with me father, mother, brothers, wife, children, friends, and all whom I value, and then I will be saved; but it is not yet the fitting time for me to be made bright;” for if you say so, there is reason to fear lest you should have as sharers of your sorrow those whom you hoped to have as sharers of your joy.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If they will be with you, well;—but do not wait for them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is base to say, “But where is my offering for my baptism, and where is my baptismal robe, in which I shall be made bright, and where is what is wanted for the entertainment of my baptizers, that in these too I may become worthy of notice?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For, as you see, all these things are necessary, and on account of this the Grace will be lessened.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not thus trifle with great things, or allow yourself to think so basely.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Sacrament is greater than the visible environment.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Offer yourself; clothe yourself with Christ, feast me with your conduct; I rejoice to be thus affectionately treated, and God Who gives these great gifts rejoices thus.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Nothing is great in the sight of God, but what the poor may give, so that the poor may not here also be outrun, for they cannot contend with the rich.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In other matters there is a distinction between poor and rich, but here the more willing is the richer.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let nothing hinder you from going on, nor draw you away from your readiness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: While your desire is still vehement, seize upon that which you desire.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: While the iron is hot, let it be tempered by the cold water, lest anything should happen in the interval, and put an end to your desire.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I am Philip; do you be Candace’s Eunuch.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you also say, “See, here is water, what doth hinder me to be baptized?”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Seize the opportunity; rejoice greatly in the blessing; and having spoken be baptized; and having been baptized be saved; and though you be an Ethiopian body, be made white in soul.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not say, “A Bishop shall baptize me,—and he a Metropolitan,—and he of Jerusalem (for the Grace does not come of a place, but of the Spirit),—and he of noble birth, for it would be a sad thing for my nobility to be insulted by being baptized by a man of no family.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not say, “I do not mind a mere Priest, if he is a celibate, and a religious, and of angelic life; for it would be a sad thing for me to be defiled even in the moment of my cleansing.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not ask for credentials of the preacher or the baptizer.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For another is his judge, and the examiner of what thou canst not see.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For man looketh on the outward appearance, but the Lord looketh on the heart.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But to thee let every one be trustworthy for purification, so only he is one of those who have been approved, not of those who are openly condemned, and not a stranger to the Church.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not judge your judges, you who need healing; and do not make nice distinctions about the rank of those who shall cleanse you, or be critical about your spiritual fathers.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: One may be higher or lower than another, but all are higher than you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Look at it this way.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: One may be golden, another iron, but both are rings and have engraved on them the same royal image; and thus when they impress the wax, what difference is there between the seal of the one and that of the other?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Detect the material in the wax, if you are so very clever.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Tell me which is the impression of the iron ring, and which of the golden.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And how do they come to be one?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The difference is in the material and not in the seal.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And so anyone can be your baptizer; for though one may excel another in his life, yet the grace of baptism is the same, and any one may be your consecrator who is formed in the same faith.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not disdain to be baptized with a poor man, if you are rich; or if you are noble, with one who is lowborn; or if you are a master, with one who is up to the present time your slave.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Not even so will you be humbling yourself as Christ, unto Whom you are baptized today, Who for your sake took upon Himself even the form of a slave.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: From the day of your new birth all the old marks were effaced, and Christ was put upon all in one form.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not disdain to confess your sins, knowing how John baptized, that by present shame you may escape from future shame (for this too is a part of the future punishment); and prove that you really hate sin by making a shew of it openly, and triumphing over it as worthy of contempt.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not reject the medicine of exorcism, nor refuse it because of its length.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This too is a touchstone of your right disposition for grace.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What labour have you to do compared with that of the Queen of Ethiopia, who arose and came from the utmost part of the earth to see the wisdom of Solomon?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And behold a Greater than Solomon is here in the judgment of those who reason maturely.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not hesitate either at length of journey, or distance by sea; or fire, if this too lies before you; or of any other, small or great, of the hindrances that you may attain to the gift.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if without any labour and trouble at all you may obtain that which you desire, what folly it is to put off the gift: “Ho, every one that thirsteth, come ye to the waters,” Esaias invites you, “and he that hath no money, come buy wine and milk, without money and without price.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: O swiftness of His mercy: O easiness of the Covenant: This blessing may be bought by you merely for willing it; He accepts the very desire as a great price; He thirsts to be thirsted for; He gives to drink to all who desire to drink; He takes it as a kindness to be asked for the kindness; He is ready and liberal; He gives with more pleasure than others receive.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Only let us not be condemned for frivolity by asking for little, and for what is unworthy of the Giver.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Blessed is he from whom Jesus asks drink, as He did from that Samaritan woman, and gives a well of water springing up unto eternal life.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Blessed is he that soweth beside all waters, and upon every soul, tomorrow to be ploughed and watered, which today the ox and the ass tread, while it is dry and without water, and oppressed with unreason.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And blessed is he who, though he be a “valley of rushes,” is watered out of the House of the Lord; for he is made fruitbearing instead of rushbearing, and produces that which is for the food of man, not that which is rough and unprofitable.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And for the sake of this we must be very careful not to miss the Grace.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Be it so, some will say, in the case of those who ask for Baptism; what have you to say about those who are still children, and conscious neither of the loss nor of the grace?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Are we to baptize them too?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Certainly, if any danger presses.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is better that they should be unconsciously sanctified than that they should depart unsealed and uninitiated.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: A proof of this is found in the Circumcision on the eighth day, which was a sort of typical seal, and was conferred on children before they had the use of reason.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And so is the anointing of the doorposts, which preserved the firstborn, though applied to things which had no consciousness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But in respect of others I give my advice to wait till the end of the third year, or a little more or less, when they may be able to listen and to answer something about the Sacrament; that, even though they do not perfectly understand it, yet at any rate they may know the outlines; and then to sanctify them in soul and body with the great sacrament of our consecration.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For this is how the matter stands; at that time they begin to be responsible for their lives, when reason is matured, and they learn the mystery of life (for of sins of ignorance owing to their tender years they have no account to give), and it is far more profitable on all accounts to be fortified by the Font, because of the sudden assaults of danger that befall us, stronger than our helpers.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But, one says, Christ was thirty years old when He was baptized, and that although He was God; and do you bid us hurry our Baptism?—You have solved the difficulty when you say He was God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For He was absolute cleansing; He had no need of cleansing; but it was for you that He was purified, just as it was for you that, though He had not flesh, yet He is clothed with flesh.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Nor was there any danger to Him from putting off Baptism, for He had the ordering of His own Passion as of His own Birth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But in your case the danger is to no small interests, if you were to depart after a birth to corruption alone, and without being clothed with incorruption.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And there is this further point for me to consider, that that particular time of baptism was a necessity for Him, but your case is not the same.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He manifested Himself in the thirtieth year after His birth and not before; first, in order that He might not appear ostentatious, which is a condition belonging to vulgar minds; and next, because that age tests virtue thoroughly, and is the right time to teach.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And since it was needful for Him to undergo the passion which saves the world, it was needful also that all things which belong to the passion should fit into the passion; the Manifestation, the Baptism, the Witness from Heaven, the Proclamation, the concourse of the multitude, the Miracles; and that they should be as it were one body, not torn asunder, nor broken apart by intervals.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For out of the Baptism and Proclamation arose that earthquake of people coming together, for so Scripture calls that time; and out of the multitude arose the shewing of the signs and the miracles that lead up to the Gospel.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And out of these came the jealousy, and from this the hatred, and out of the hatred the circumstance of the plot against Him, and the betrayal; and out of these the Cross, and the other events by which our Salvation has been effected.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such are the reasons in the case of Christ so far as we can attain to them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And perhaps another more secret reason might be found.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But for you, what necessity is there that by following the examples which are far above you, you should do a thing so ill-advised for yourself?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For there are many other details of the Gospel History which are quite different to what happens nowadays, and the seasons of which do not correspond.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For instance Christ fasted a little before His temptation, we before Easter.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: As far as the fasting days are concerned it is the same, but the difference in the seasons is no little one.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He armed Himself with them against temptation; but to us this fast is symbolical of dying with Christ, and it is a purification in preparation for the festival.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And He fasted absolutely for forty days, for He was God; but we measure our fasting by our power, even though some are led by zeal to rush beyond their strength.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Again, He gave the Sacrament of the Passover to His Disciples in an upper chamber, and after supper, and one day before He suffered; but we celebrate it in Houses of Prayer, and before food, and after His resurrection.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He rose again the third day; our resurrection is not till after a long time.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But matters which have to do with Him are neither abruptly separated from us, nor yet yoked together with those which concern us in point of time; but they were handed down to us just so far as to be patterns of what we should do, and then they carefully avoided an entire and exact resemblance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If then you will listen to me, you will bid a long farewell to all such arguments, and you will jump at this Blessing, and begin to struggle in a twofold conflict; first, to prepare yourself for baptism by purifying yourself; and next, to preserve the baptismal gift; for it is a matter of equal difficulty to obtain a blessing which we have not, and to keep it when we have gained it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For often what zeal has acquired sloth has destroyed; and what hesitation has lost diligence has regained.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: A great assistance to the attainment of what you desire are vigils, fasts, sleeping on the ground, prayers, tears, pity of and almsgiving to those who are in need.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And let these be your thanksgiving for what you have received, and at the same time your safeguard of them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You have the benefit to remind you of many commandments; so do not transgress them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Does a poor man approach you?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Remember how poor you once were, and how rich you were made.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: One in want of bread or of drink, perhaps another Lazarus, is cast at your gate; respect the Sacramental Table to which you have approached, the Bread of Which you have partaken, the Cup in Which you have communicated, being consecrated by the Sufferings of Christ.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If a stranger fall at your feet, homeless and a foreigner, welcome in him Him who for your sake was a stranger, and that among His own, and who came to dwell in you by His grace, and who drew you towards the heavenly dwelling place.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Be a Zaccheus, who yesterday was a Publican, and is to-day of liberal soul; offer all to the coming in of Christ, that though small in bodily stature you may show yourself great, nobly contemplating Christ.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: A sick or a wounded man lies before you; respect your own health, and the wounds from which Christ delivered you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you see one naked clothe him, in honour of your own garment of incorruption, which is Christ, for as many as were baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you find a debtor falling at your feet, tear up every document, whether just or unjust.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Remember the ten thousand talents which Christ forgave you, and be not a harsh exactor of a smaller debt—and that from whom?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: From your fellow servant, you who were forgiven so much more by the Master.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Otherwise you will have to give satisfaction to His mercy, which you would not imitate and take as your copy.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let the laver be not for your body only, but also for the image of God in you; not merely a washing away of sins in you, but also a correction of your temper; let it not only wash away the old filth, but let it purify the fountainhead.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let it not only move you to honourable acquisition, but let it teach you also honourably to lose possession; or, which is more easy, to make restitution of what you have wrongfully acquired.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For what profit is it that your sin should have been forgiven you, but the loss which you have inflicted should not be repaired to him whom you have injured?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
