verses.text: Two sins are on your conscience, the one that you made a dishonest gain, the other that you retained the gains; you received forgiveness for the one, but in respect of the other you are still in sin, for you have still possession of what belongs to another; and your sin has not been put to an end, but only divided by the time which has elapsed.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Part of it was perpetrated before your Baptism, but part remains after your Baptism; for Baptism carries forgiveness of Past, not of Present sins; and its purification must not be played with, but be genuinely impressed upon you; you must be made perfectly bright, and not be merely coloured; you must receive the gift, not of a mere covering of your sins, but of a taking them clean away.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven…this is done by the complete cleansing…and whose sins are hidden…this belongs to those who are not yet healed in their deepest soul.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Blessed is the man to whom the Lord will not impute sin.…This is a third class of sinners, whose actions are not praiseworthy, but who are innocent of intention.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What say I then, and what is my argument?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yesterday you were a Canaanite soul bent together by sin; today you have been made straight by the Word.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not be bent gain, and condemned to the earth, as if weighed down by the Devil with a wooden collar, nor get an incurable curvature.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yesterday you were being dried up by an abundant hæmorrhage, for you were pouring out crimson sin; today stanched and flourishing again, for you have touched the hem of Christ and your issue has been stayed.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Guard, I pray you, the cleansing lest you should again have a hæmorrhage, and not be able to lay hold of Christ to steal salvation; for Christ does not like to be stolen from often, though He is very merciful.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yesterday you were flung upon a bed, exhausted and paralyzed, and you had no one when the water should be troubled to put you into the pool.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Today you have Him Who is in one Person Man and God, or rather God and Man.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You were raised up from your bed, or rather you took up your bed, and publicly acknowledged the benefit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not again be thrown upon your bed by sinning, in the evil rest of a body paralyzed by its pleasures.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But as you now are, so walk, mindful of the command, Behold thou art made whole; sin no more lest a worse thing happen unto thee if thou prove thyself bad after the blessing thou hast received.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You have heard the loud voice, Lazarus, come forth, as you lay in the tomb; not, however, after four days, but after many days; and you were loosed from the bonds of your graveclothes.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not again become dead, nor live with those who dwell in the tombs; nor bind yourself with the bonds of your own sins; for it is uncertain whether you will rise again from the tomb till the last and universal resurrection, which will bring every work into judgment, not to be healed, but to be judged, and to give account of all which for good or evil it has treasured up.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you were full of leprosy, that shapeless evil, yet you scraped off the evil matter, and received again the Image whole.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Shew your cleansing to me your Priest, that I may recognize how much more precious it is than the legal one.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not range yourself with the nine unthankful men, but imitate the tenth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For although he was a Samaritan, yet he was of better mind than the others.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Make certain that you will not break out again with evil ulcers, and find the indisposition of your body hard to heal.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yesterday meanness and avarice were withering your hand; to-day let liberality and kindness stretch it out.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is a noble cure for a weak hand to disperse abroad, to give to the poor, to pour out the things which we possess abundantly, till we reach the very bottom; and perhaps this will gush forth food for you, as for the woman of Sarepta, and especially if you happen to be feeding an Elias, to recognize that it is a good abundance to be needy for the sake of Christ, Who for our sakes became poor.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you were deaf and dumb, let the Word sound in your ears, or rather keep there Him Who hath sounded.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not shut your ears to the Instruction of the Lord, and to His Counsel, like the adder to charms.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you are blind and unenlightened, lighten your eyes that you sleep not in death.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In God’s Light see light, and in the Spirit of God be enlightened by the Son, That Threefold and Undivided Light.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you receive all the Word, you will bring therewith upon your own soul all the healing powers of Christ, with which separately these individuals were healed.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Only be not ignorant of the measure of grace; only let not the enemy, while you sleep, maliciously sow tares.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Only take care that as by your cleansing you have become an object of enmity to the Evil One, you do not again make yourself an object of pity by sin.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Only be careful lest, while rejoicing and lifted up above measure by the blessing, you fall again through pride.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Only be diligent as to your cleansing, “setting ascensions in your heart,” and keep with all diligence the remission which you have received as a gift, in order that, while the remission comes from God, the preservation of it may come from yourself also.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How shall this be?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Remember always the parable, and so will you best and most perfectly help yourself.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The unclean and malignant spirit is gone out of you, being chased by baptism.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He will not submit to the expulsion, he will not resign himself to be houseless and homeless: He goes through waterless places, dry of the Divine Stream, and there he desires to abide.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He wanders, seeking rest; he finds none.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He lights on baptized souls, whose sins the font has washed away.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He fears the water; he is choked with the cleansing, as the Legion were in the sea.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Again he returns to the house whence he came out.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He is shameless, he is contentious, he makes a fresh assault upon it, he makes a new attempt.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If he finds that Christ has taken up His abode there, and has filled the place which he had vacated, he is driven back again, and goes off without success and is become an object of pity in his wandering state.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if he finds in you a place, swept and garnished indeed, but empty and idle, equally ready to take in this or that which shall first occupy it, he makes a leap into it, he takes up his abode there with a larger train; and the last state is worse than the first, inasmuch as then there was a hope of amendment and safety, but now the evil is rampant, and drags in sin by its flight from good, and therefore the possession is more secure to him who dwells there.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will remind you again about Illuminations, and that often, and will reckon them up from Holy Scripture.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For I myself shall be happier for remembering them (for what is sweeter than light to those who have tasted light?) and I will dazzle you with my words.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: There is sprung up a light for the righteous, and its partner joyful gladness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And, The light of the righteous is everlasting; and Thou art shining wondrously from the everlasting mountains, is said to God, I think of the Angelic powers which aid our efforts after good.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And you have heard David’s words; The Lord is my Light and my Salvation, whom then shall I fear?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And now he asks that the Light and the Truth may be sent forth for him, now giving thanks that he has a share in it, in that the Light of God is marked upon him; that is, that the signs of the illumination given are impressed upon him and recognized.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: One light alone let us shun—that which is the offspring of the baleful fire; let us not walk in the light of our fire, and in the flame which we have kindled.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For I know a cleansing fire which Christ came to send upon the earth, and He Himself is anagogically called a Fire.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This Fire takes away whatsoever is material and of evil habit; and this He desires to kindle with all speed, for He longs for speed in doing us good, since He gives us even coals of fire to help us.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I know also a fire which is not cleansing, but avenging; either that fire of Sodom which He pours down on all sinners, mingled with brimstone and storms, or that which is prepared for the Devil and his Angels or that which proceeds from the face of the Lord, and shall burn up his enemies round about; and one even more fearful still than these, the unquenchable fire which is ranged with the worm that dieth not but is eternal for the wicked.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For all these belong to the destroying power; though some may prefer even in this place to take a more merciful view of this fire, worthily of Him That chastises.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And as I know of two kinds of fire, so also do I of light.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The one is the light of our ruling power directing our steps according to the will of God; the other is a deceitful and meddling one, quite contrary to the true light, though pretending to be that light, that it may cheat us by its appearance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This really is darkness, yet has the appearance of noonday, the high perfection of light.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And so I read that passage of those who continually flee in darkness at noonday; for this is really night, and yet is thought to be bright light by those who have been ruined by luxury.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For what saith David?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: “Night was around me and I knew it not, for I thought that my luxury was enlightenment.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But such are they, and in this condition; but let us kindle for ourselves the light of knowledge.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This will be done by sowing unto righteousness, and reaping the fruit of life, for action is the patron of contemplation, that amongst other things we may learn also what is the true light, and what the false, and be saved from falling unawares into evil wearing the guise of good.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us be made light, as it was said to the disciples by the Great Light, ye are the light of the world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us be made lights in the world, holding forth the Word of Life; that is, let us be made a quickening power to others.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us lay hold of the Godhead; let us lay hold of the First and Brightest Light.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us walk towards Him shining, before our feet stumble upon dark and hostile mountains.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: While it is day let us walk honestly as in the day, not in rioting and drunkenness, not in chambering and wantonness, which are the dishonesties of the night.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us cleanse every member, Brethren, let us purify every sense; let nothing in us be imperfect or of our first birth; let us leave nothing unilluminated.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us enlighten our eyes, that we may look straight on, and not bear in ourselves any harlot idol through curious and busy sight; for even though we might not worship lust, yet our soul would be defiled.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If there be beam or mote, let us purge it away, that we may be able to see those of others also.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us be enlightened in our ears; let us be enlightened in our tongue, that we may hearken what the Lord God will speak, and that He may cause us to hear His lovingkindness in the morning, and that we may be made to hear of joy and gladness, spoken into godly ears, that we may not be a sharp sword, nor a whetted razor, nor turn under our tongue labour and toil, but that we may speak the Wisdom of God in a mystery, even the hidden Wisdom, reverencing the fiery tongues.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us be healed also in the smell, that we be not effeminate; and be sprinkled with dust instead of sweet perfumes, but may smell the Ointment that was poured out for us, spiritually receiving it; and so formed and transformed by it, that from us too a sweet odour may be smelled.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us cleanse our touch, our taste, our throat, not touching them over gently, nor delighting in smooth things, but handling them as is worthy of Him, the Word That was made flesh for us; and so far following the example of Thomas, not pampering them with dainties and sauces, those brethren of a more baleful pampering, but tasting and learning that the Lord is good, with the better and abiding taste; and not for a short while refreshing that baneful and thankless dust, which lets pass and does not hold that which is given to it; but delighting it with the words which are sweeter than honey.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And in addition to what has been said, it is good with our head cleansed, as the head which is the workshop of the senses is cleansed, to hold fast the Head of Christ, from which the whole body is fitly joined together and compacted; and to cast down our sin that exalted itself, when it would exalt us above our better part.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is good also for the shoulder to be sanctified and purified that it may be able to take up the Cross of Christ, which not everyone can easily do.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is good for the hands to be consecrated, and the feet; the one that they may in every place be lifted up holy; and that they may lay hold of the discipline of Christ, lest the Lord at any time be angered; and that the Word may gain credence by action, as was the case with that which was given in the hand of a prophet; the other, that they be not swift to shed blood, nor to run to evil, but that they be prompt to run to the Gospel and the Prize of the high Calling, and to receive Christ Who washes and cleanses them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if there be also a cleansing of that belly which receiveth and digesteth the food of the Word, it were good also; not to make it a god by luxury and the meat that perisheth, but rather to give it all possible cleansing, and to make it more spare, that it may receive the Word of God at the very heart, and grieve honourably over the sins of Israel.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I find also the heart and inward parts deemed worthy of honour.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: David convinces me of this, when he prays that a clean heart may be created in him, and a right spirit renewed in his inward parts; meaning, I think, the mind and its movements or thoughts.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And what of the loins, or reins, for we must not pass these over?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let the purification take hold of these also.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let our loins be girded about and kept in check by continence, as the Law bade Israel of old when partaking of the Passover.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For none comes out of Egypt purely, or escapes the Destroyer, except he who has disciplined these.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And let the reins be changed by that good conversion by which they transfer all the affections to God, so that they can say, Lord, all my desire is before Thee, and the day of man have I not desired; for you must be a man of desires, but they must be those of the spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For thus you would destroy the dragon that carries the greater part of his strength upon his navel and his loins, by slaying the power that comes to him from these.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do not be surprised at my giving a more abundant honour to our uncomely parts, mortifying them and making them chaste by my speech, and standing up against the flesh.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us give to God all our members which are upon the earth; let us consecrate them all; not the lobe of the liver or the kidneys with the fat, nor some part of our bodies now this now that (why should we despise the rest?); but let us bring ourselves entire, let us be reasonable holocausts, perfect sacrifices; and let us not make only the shoulder or the breast a portion for the Priest to take away, for that would be a small thing, but let us give ourselves entire, that we may receive back ourselves entire; for this is to receive entirely, when we give ourselves to God and offer as a sacrifice our own salvation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Besides all this and before all, keep I pray you the good deposit, by which I live and work, and which I desire to have as the companion of my departure; with which I endure all that is so distressful, and despise all delights; the confession of the Father and the Son and the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This I commit unto you to-day; with this I will baptize you and make you grow.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This I give you to share, and to defend all your life, the One Godhead and Power, found in the Three in Unity, and comprising the Three separately, not unequal, in substances or natures, neither increased nor diminished by superiorities or inferiorities; in every respect equal, in every respect the same; just as the beauty and the greatness of the heavens is one; the infinite conjunction of Three Infinite Ones, Each God when considered in Himself; as the Father so the Son, as the Son so the Holy Ghost; the Three One God when contemplated together; Each God because Consubstantial; One God because of the Monarchia.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: No sooner do I conceive of the One than I am illumined by the Splendour of the Three; no sooner do I distinguish Them than I am carried back to the One.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: When I think of any One of the Three I think of Him as the Whole, and my eyes are filled, and the greater part of what I am thinking of escapes me.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I cannot grasp the greatness of That One so as to attribute a greater greatness to the Rest.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: When I contemplate the Three together, I see but one torch, and cannot divide or measure out the Undivided Light.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you fear to speak of Generation lest you should attribute aught of passion to the impassible God?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I on the other hand fear to speak of Creation, lest I should destroy God by the insult and the untrue division, either cutting the Son away from the Father, or from the Son the Substance of the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For this paradox is involved, that not only is a created Life foisted into the Godhead by those who measure Godhead badly; but even this created life is divided against itself.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For as these low earthly minds make the Son subject to the Father, so again is the rank of the Spirit made inferior to that of the Son, until both God and created life are insulted by the new Theology.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: No, my friends, there is nothing servile in the Trinity, nothing created, nothing accidental, as I have heard one of the wise say.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If I yet pleased men I should not be the servant of Christ, says the Apostle; and if I yet worshipped a creature, or were baptized into a creature, I should not be made divine, nor have changed my first birth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What shall I say to those who worship Astarte or Chemosh, the abomination of the Sidonians, or the likeness of a star, a god a little above them to these idolaters, but yet a creature and a piece of workmanship, when I myself either do not worship Two of Those into Whose united Name I am baptized, or else worship my fellow-servants, for they are fellow-servants, even if a little higher in the scale; for differences must exist among fellow-servants.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I should like to call the Father the greater, because from him flows both the Equality and the Being of the Equals (this will be granted on all hands), but I am afraid to use the word Origin, lest I should make Him the Origin of Inferiors, and thus insult Him by precedencies of honour.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the lowering of those Who are from Him is no glory to the Source.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Moreover, I look with suspicion at your insatiate desire, for fear you should take hold of this word Greater, and divide the Nature, using the word Greater in all senses, whereas it does not apply to the Nature, but only to Origination.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For in the Consubstantial Persons there is nothing greater or less in point of Substance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I would honour the Son as Son before the Spirit, but Baptism consecrating me through the Spirit does not allow of this.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But are you afraid of being reproached with Tritheism?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you take possession of this good thing, the Unity in the Three, and leave me to fight the battle.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let me be the shipbuilder, and do you use the ship; or if another is the builder of the ship, take me for the architect of the house, and do you live in it with safety, though you spent no labour upon it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You shall not have a less prosperous voyage, or a less safe habitation than I who built them, because you have not laboured upon them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: See how great is my indulgence; see the goodness of the Spirit; the war shall be mine, yours the achievement; I will be under fire, and you shall live in peace; but join with your defender in prayer, and give me your hand by the Faith.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I have three stones which I will sling at the Philistine; I have three inspirations against the son of the Sareptan, with which I will quicken the slain; I have three floods against the faggots with which I will consecrate the Sacrifice with water, raising the most unexpected fire; and I will throw down the prophets of shame by the power of the Sacrament.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What need have I any more of speech?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is the time for teaching, not for controversy.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I protest before God and the elect Angels, be thou baptized in this faith.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If thy heart is written upon in some other way than as my teaching demands, come and have the writing changed; I am no unskilled caligrapher of these truths.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I write that which is written upon my own heart; and I teach that which I have been taught, and have kept from the beginning up to these hoar hairs.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Mine is the risk; be mine also the reward of being the Director of your soul, and consecrating you by Baptism.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if you are already rightly disposed, and marked with the good inscription, see that you keep what is written, and remain unchanged in a changing time concerning an unchanging Thing.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Follow Pilate’s example in the better sense; you who are rightly written on, imitate him who wrote wrongfully.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Say to those who would persuade you differently, what I have written, I have written.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For indeed I should be ashamed if, while that which was wrong remained inflexible, that which is right should be so easily bent aside; whereas we ought to be easily bent to that which is better from that which is worse, but immovable from the better to the worse.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If it be thus, and according to this teaching that you come to Baptism, lo I will not refrain my lips, lo I lend my hands to the Spirit; let us hasten your salvation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Spirit is eager, the Consecrator is ready, the Gift is prepared.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if you still halt and will not receive the perfectness of the Godhead, go and look for someone else to baptize—or rather to drown you: I have no time to cut the Godhead, and to make you dead in the moment of your regeneration, that you should have neither the Gift nor the Hope of Grace, but should in so short a time make shipwreck of your salvation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For whatever you may subtract from the Deity of the Three, you will have overthrown the whole, and destroyed your own being made perfect.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But not yet perhaps is there formed upon your soul any writing good or bad; and you want to be written upon today, and formed by us unto perfection.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us go within the cloud.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Give me the tables of your heart; I will be your Moses, though this be a bold thing to say; I will write on them with the finger of God a new Decalogue.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will write on them a shorter method of salvation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if there be any heretical or unreasoning beast, let him remain below, or he will run the risk of being stoned by the Word of truth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will baptize you and make you a disciple in the Name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost; and These Three have One common name, the Godhead.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And you shall know, both by appearances and by words that you reject all ungodliness, and are united to all the Godhead.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Believe that all that is in the world, both all that is seen and all that is unseen, was made out of nothing by God, and is governed by the Providence of its Creator, and will receive a change to a better state.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Believe that evil has no substance or kingdom, either unoriginate or self-existent or created by God; but that it is our work, and the evil one’s, and came upon us through our heedlessness, but not from our Creator.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Believe that the Son of God, the Eternal Word, Who was begotten of the Father before all time and without body, was in these latter days for your sake made also Son of Man, born of the Virgin Mary ineffably and stainlessly (for nothing can be stained where God is, and by which salvation comes), in His own Person at once entire Man and perfect God, for the sake of the entire sufferer, that He may bestow salvation on your whole being, having destroyed the whole condemnation of your sins: impassible in His Godhead, passible in that which He assumed; as much Man for your sake as you are made God for His.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Believe that for us sinners He was led to death; was crucified and buried, so far as to taste of death; and that He rose again the third day, and ascended into heaven, that He might take you with Him who were lying low; and that He will come again with His glorious Presence to judge the quick and the dead; no longer flesh, nor yet without a body, according to the laws which He alone knows of a more godlike body, that He may be seen by those who pierced Him, and on the other hand may remain as God without carnality.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Receive besides this the Resurrection, the Judgment and the Reward according to the righteous scales of God; and believe that this will be Light to those whose mind is purified (that is, God—seen and known) proportionate to their degree of purity, which we call the Kingdom of heaven; but to those who suffer from blindness of their ruling faculty, darkness, that is estrangement from God, proportionate to their blindness here.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Then, in the tenth place, work that which is good upon this foundation of dogma; for faith without works is dead, even as are works apart from faith.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This is all that may be divulged of the Sacrament, and that is not forbidden to the ear of the many.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The rest you shall learn within the Church by the grace of the Holy Trinity; and those matters you shall conceal within yourself, sealed and secure.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But one thing more I preach unto you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Station in which you shall presently stand after your Baptism before the Great Sanctuary is a foretype of the future glory.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Psalmody with which you will be received is a prelude to the Psalmody of Heaven; the lamps which you will kindle are a Sacrament of the illumination there with which we shall meet the Bridegroom, shining and virgin souls, with the lamps of our faith shining, not sleeping through our carelessness, that we may not miss Him that we look for if He come unexpectedly; nor yet unfed, and without oil, and destitute of good works, that we be not cast out of the Bridechamber.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For I see how pitiable is such a case.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He will come when the cry demands the meeting, and they who are prudent shall meet Him, with their light shining and its food abundant, but the others seeking for oil too late from those who possess it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And He will come with speed, and the former shall go in with Him, but the latter shall be shut out, having wasted in preparations the time of entrance; and they shall weep sore when all too late they learn the penalty of their slothfulness, when the Bride-chamber can no longer be entered by them for all their entreaties, for they have shut it against themselves by their sin, following in another fashion the example of those who missed the Wedding feast with which the good Father feasts the good Bridegroom; one on account of a newly wedded wife; another of a newly purchased field; another of a yoke of oxen; which he and they acquired to their misfortune, since for the sake of the little they lose the great.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For none are there of the disdainful, nor of the slothful, nor of those who are clothed in filthy rags and not in the Wedding garment even though here they may have thought themselves worthy of wearing the bright robe there, and secretly intruded themselves, deceiving themselves with vain hopes.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And then, What?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: When we have entered, then the Bridegroom knows what He will teach us, and how He will converse with the souls that have come in with Him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He will converse with them, I think in teaching things more perfect and more pure.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Of which may we all, both Teachers and Taught, have share, in the Same Christ our Lord, to Whom be the Glory and the Empire, for ever and ever.
